title: gahnia penangensis
intro: Gahnia penangensis, also known as the Malayan saw sedge or swamp saw sedge, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various infections. Gahnia penangensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its herbal preparations are commonly used in traditional medicine in Southeast Asia.
